title: put with ?field= silently destroys other fields — not in user-facing docs
description:
Trigger: user writes two fields to a Vault secret via put(?field=a) then put(?field=b); field a is silently gone.

KV v2 set replaces the entire data map with each write. The new code documents this via an inline comment in put(), but the module-level doc (what users read) only says put writes '{field: value} as the KV data map' — which a careful reader understands but a typical user will read as 'adds/updates field in the KV map.'

The maintenance consequence: users will file issues or silently lose data when rotating a second field on a secret that already has fields. The fix is one sentence in the module-level ?field= doc bullet: 'each put creates a new version containing only the specified field; previously written fields in the same secret are not carried forward.'
